name: n8n
tagline: The most-starred repo here that isn't open source
description: A fair-code workflow automation platform combining visual node-based building with custom code, 400+ integrations, and native AI and agent capabilities, self-hostable or cloud.
why: Enormously popular and constantly described as open source — but n8n itself uses the term 'fair-code', not open source. The Sustainable Use License limits use to your own internal business, non-commercial, or personal purposes, and forbids hosting n8n as a commercial service.
useCases: Internal automation and AI agent workflows
audience: Ops and dev teams automating internal processes

setup.requirements: npx or Docker one-liner; production wants Postgres and more

license.code: Sustainable Use License + n8n Enterprise License (fair-code, not OSI)
license.notes: Free to self-host internally, but not open source: you can't offer it as a commercial service, and enterprise features are gated.

limitations: The license restricts commercial hosting and redistribution — fine for internal use, disqualifying for the open-source label.
take: Excellent software, and genuinely generous terms for most users. Just not open source — for an MIT visual builder, see Langflow.
